items.title: Explore Microsoft Agent Framework Orchestration Patterns 1.0 GA
items.description: Microsoft Agent Framework's orchestration layer has reached 1.0 across both Python and .NET. Sequential, concurrent, group chat, handoff, and magentic orchestration patterns are now stable in both SDKs — no more experimental gates. Magentic is the standout: give it a goal, a manager, and a set of specialists and it self-organises. Study the patterns, run the samples, and evaluate which fits your multi-agent architecture.

items.title: Explore Agent Skills for Python in Microsoft Agent Framework (GA)
items.description: Agent Skills for Python is now stable and production-ready in Microsoft Agent Framework (July 15 2026). Skills are reusable bundles of domain expertise — instructions, reference documents, and scripts — that agents load only when a task requires them. Teams can author and ship skills independently then compose them into any agent. Complements the earlier .NET Skills GA. Build a skill and attach it to a Python agent to test the full authoring flow.

items.title: Lab: Agentic Autofix for GitHub Code Scanning Alerts (Public Preview)
items.description: GitHub Copilot Agentic Autofix entered public preview on July 10 2026 for all code scanning alerts (CodeQL and third-party). It works like a developer: explores relevant files, proposes a fix, reruns the original analysis to confirm the alert is closed, then opens a pull request. Enable it on a repository with code scanning, trigger an alert, and observe the full agentic remediation loop.

items.title: Explore Microsoft Foundry Agent Service: Publish Agents to M365 Copilot and Teams
items.description: June 2026 Foundry What's New (updated July 15) adds the ability to publish Foundry-built agents directly to Microsoft 365 Copilot and Microsoft Teams, including in virtual-network deployments. Memory in Foundry Agent Service also entered preview, and a new agent optimizer quickstart enables hosted-agent tuning. Review the new docs, build a simple Foundry agent, and publish it to a Teams channel to validate the end-to-end flow.

items.title: Explore MCP Enterprise Authorization with Microsoft Entra and Azure App Service
items.description: Published July 16 2026, this App Service team deep-dive covers how to add enterprise-grade authorization to MCP servers today using Microsoft Entra ID and App Service built-in auth. Covers Entra app registrations, OAuth scopes, and App Service Easy Auth to secure MCP tool endpoints without custom middleware. Essential reading for teams exposing agentic tools inside corporate boundaries.
